evals.prompt: Write eval cases for the code-knowledge skill.
evals.expected_output: The agent should trigger skill-eval generation mode, read the target skill, and plan to create or update code-knowledge eval cases.
expectations: Triggered the skill-eval skill
Planned to read the target skill's SKILL.md before drafting cases
Planned to write or update skills/code-knowledge/evals/evals.json
Did NOT plan to run the eval suite

evals.prompt: Complete the missing evals for web-page-debugger.
evals.expected_output: The agent should treat this as eval case generation for web-page-debugger and cover trigger boundaries.
expectations: Triggered the skill-eval skill
Planned to generate or complete eval cases for web-page-debugger
Planned to cover positive triggers, negative triggers, and workflow constraints
Did NOT ask the user which agent CLI to use

evals.prompt: Run the design-system skill evals and test routing accuracy.
evals.expected_output: The agent should trigger skill-eval run mode and plan a routing-mode eval run for design-system.
expectations: Triggered the skill-eval skill
Planned to run the design-system eval suite
Planned to use routing mode by default
Planned to notify the user that running evals invokes LLMs and may cost money

evals.prompt: Evaluate whether prompts are triggering the wrong skill for code-knowledge.
evals.expected_output: The agent should treat this as a skill routing evaluation request for code-knowledge.
expectations: Triggered the skill-eval skill
Planned to evaluate skill trigger accuracy for code-knowledge
Planned to use the existing eval file if present
Planned to report case-level PASS/FAIL results after execution

evals.prompt: I changed code-knowledge. Finish the task.
evals.expected_output: The agent should not run evals merely because a skill changed, but should ensure eval cases are updated when the change is a skill modification.
expectations: Triggered the skill-eval skill only for generating or updating eval cases, not for executing evals
Did NOT plan to run LLM-backed evals without an explicit run request
Planned to update skills/code-knowledge/evals/evals.json if the skill behavior changed

evals.prompt: Lint the assets after my rule change.
evals.expected_output: The agent should not trigger skill-eval because the user asked for asset linting, not skill eval generation or execution.
expectations: Did NOT trigger the skill-eval skill
Planned to run asset linting instead of skill eval generation
Did NOT plan to write an evals.json file

evals.prompt: Use the web-page-debugger to check localhost:3000.
evals.expected_output: The agent should route to web-page-debugger rather than skill-eval.
expectations: Did NOT trigger the skill-eval skill
Rejected skill-eval as unrelated to running or generating skill evals
Planned to use web-page-debugger or browser-based verification

evals.prompt: Can you add tests for the CLI asset scanner?
evals.expected_output: The agent should treat this as normal repository test work, not skill evaluation, unless the user specifically asks for skill eval cases.
expectations: Did NOT trigger the skill-eval skill
Planned normal CLI or repository tests instead of skill eval cases
Did NOT plan to invoke scripts/skill-eval/scripts/run.js

evals.prompt: Run skill-eval for skill-eval itself, but keep the artifacts so I can inspect transcripts.
evals.expected_output: The agent should plan a routing eval run for skill-eval and retain artifacts outside skills/skill-eval/evals/runs.
expectations: Triggered the skill-eval skill
Planned to run the skill-eval eval suite
Planned to use --keep-runs
Did NOT plan to retain artifacts under skills/skill-eval/evals/runs

evals.prompt: ui-design 有几个 routing eval 没过，是不是把这些触发词都补到 description 里？
evals.expected_output: The agent should trigger skill-eval, reject description stuffing as the default repair, classify failures into boundary, wiring, coverage, description category gap, trigger layering, workflow, or ambiguity, and only adjust description when a broad primary context is truly missing.
expectations: Triggered the skill-eval skill
Did NOT default to adding every failing trigger phrase to the skill description
Planned to classify routing failures before choosing a repair
Named alternatives such as splitting or narrowing the skill, fixing installation or AGENTS.md navigation, adding eval coverage, updating the skill body, or clarifying ambiguous cases
Allowed description changes only for a missing broad primary context while respecting the description budget

evals.prompt: skill 当然要写触发条件吧？如果 routing 不稳，是不是应该把触发条件分层写清楚？
evals.expected_output: The agent should trigger skill-eval and treat layered trigger guidance as a valid repair: description holds broad trigger categories, SKILL.md body holds full when-to-use and when-not-to-use semantics, and evals hold realistic prompt regressions.
expectations: Triggered the skill-eval skill
Confirmed that skills can and should include trigger conditions
Planned to layer trigger guidance instead of removing trigger conditions
Assigned broad trigger categories to the frontmatter description
Assigned detailed trigger semantics and boundaries to the SKILL.md body
Assigned realistic positive, negative, or ambiguous prompts to eval cases

evals.prompt: 自审一下所有 skills 的 description，优化后跑一遍 routing eval。
evals.expected_output: The agent should trigger skill-eval, review descriptions as discovery metadata, update eval cases when skill behavior changes, then run routing evals because the user explicitly asked to execute them.
expectations: Triggered the skill-eval skill
Reviewed skill descriptions as discovery metadata
Planned to update eval cases for modified skills
Planned to run routing evals because the user explicitly requested execution
Did not treat eval execution as implied merely by skill changes
